faqs.question: What future destinations is SpaceWing planning?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing's ambitious roadmap includes: Mars Transit Mission (projected 2030), offering orbital flyby and potential surface landing; Venus Atmospheric Station (projected 2032), a floating habitat in Venus's upper atmosphere; Asteroid Mining Experience (projected 2035), visiting near-Earth asteroids; and Lagrange Point Hotels, permanent stations at stable orbital points. Interest lists are open now with refundable deposits.

faqs.question: When will Mars missions be available?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing's Mars Transit Program is projected to launch in 2030. We're currently in the advanced development phase with our MarsGlider spacecraft. Interest list registration is open with a fully refundable $50,000 deposit. Mars missions will include approximately 6-month transit time each way, Mars orbit, and potential surface excursion. Full pricing and details will be announced closer to launch.

faqs.question: What is the Venus Atmospheric Station?
faqs.answer: Our Venus Atmospheric Station concept (projected 2032) is a floating habitat in Venus's upper atmosphere where temperature and pressure are remarkably Earth-like. At 50km altitude, Venus offers stunning views, unique science opportunities, and a truly otherworldly experience. Interest list registration is available now.

faqs.question: Will SpaceWing offer space hotels?
faqs.answer: Yes! Our Orbital Station is just the beginning. We're developing: SpaceWing Gateway (a larger orbital complex opening 2027), Lunar Gateway Hotel (orbiting the Moon, projected 2028), and Lagrange Point stations (permanent facilities at gravitationally stable points). These will offer extended stays from weeks to months.

faqs.question: Can I invest in SpaceWing's future?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing offers investment opportunities to qualified investors through our SpaceWing Ventures program. Investment supports our future mission development and offers potential returns as we expand. Minimum investment is $100,000. Contact our Investor Relations team at investors@spacewing.com for more information.

faqs.question: How can I stay updated on future missions?
faqs.answer: Subscribe to our newsletter at spacewing.com/newsletter, follow us on social media (@SpaceWing), or join our Future Explorers program for exclusive updates and early booking opportunities. Members receive announcements before the general public, and deposit holders for future missions receive detailed progress reports.

faqs.question: What is the SpaceWing Research Program?
faqs.answer: Our Research Program allows travelers to participate in real scientific research during their missions. Current studies include: microgravity effects on materials, plant growth experiments, human physiology studies, and Earth observation research. Research participation is included in mission pricing, with no prior experience required.

faqs.question: Will there be space manufacturing opportunities?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing is developing space manufacturing facilities for: fiber optic production (superior quality in zero-G), protein crystallization for pharmaceuticals, and unique materials production. Corporate clients can arrange manufacturing missions, and individual travelers may observe and participate in demonstrations. Full services projected 2028.

faqs.question: What is the SpaceWing University program?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing University offers educational programs including: online space science courses, in-person training intensives, student research opportunities, and scholarships for exceptional students. Our goal is to inspire and prepare the next generation of space explorers. Programs are available for K-12 and university levels.

faqs.question: How does SpaceWing contribute to space exploration?
faqs.answer: Beyond tourism, SpaceWing contributes to: scientific research on every flight, technology development for future exploration, partnerships with NASA and ESA, educational outreach programs, and environmental monitoring from orbit. A portion of every ticket supports our SpaceWing Foundation, funding research and STEM education.
